close_reason: Removed :clear value filtering workarounds from peek, min_vesting_time, and queue_empty? now that core fix is in. Removed associated regression tests.
description:
## Summary

Remove the `:clear` value filtering workaround from `bedrock_job_queue` once the root cause is fixed in `bedrock_core`.

## Current Workaround

The Store module manually filters `:clear` values in three places:

### 1. `peek/4` (line ~191)
```elixir
keyspaces.items
|> repo.get_range(limit: max_scan)
|> Stream.reject(fn {_key, value} -> value == :clear end)  # Remove this
|> Stream.map(fn {_key, value} -> decode(value) end)
```

### 2. `min_vesting_time/4` (line ~508)
```elixir
keyspaces.items
|> repo.get_range(limit: limit)
|> Stream.reject(fn {_key, value} -> value == :clear end)  # Remove this
|> Enum.reduce(nil, fn {_key, value}, acc -> ... end)
```

### 3. `queue_empty?/3` (line ~620)
```elixir
keyspaces.items
|> repo.get_range(limit: 1)
|> Enum.reject(fn {_key, value} -> value == :clear end)  # Remove this
|> Enum.empty?()
```

## After Core Fix

Once bedrock-xma is resolved, these workarounds become unnecessary. Remove them and simplify the code back to:

```elixir
# peek/4
keyspaces.items
|> repo.get_range(limit: max_scan)
|> Stream.map(fn {_key, value} -> decode(value) end)

# min_vesting_time/4
keyspaces.items
|> repo.get_range(limit: limit)
|> Enum.reduce(nil, fn {_key, value}, acc -> ... end)

# queue_empty?/3
repo.get_range(keyspaces.items, limit: 1) == []
```

## Files to Modify

- `apps/bedrock_job_queue/lib/bedrock/job_queue/store.ex`

## Verification

The regression tests in `store_test.exs` should still pass after removal - they test that the Store handles `:clear` values correctly. Once the core fix is in, the Store won't receive `:clear` values anymore, so the tests will pass trivially.
title: Remove :clear value workaround after core fix
